LAW OFFICES OF

PROCOPIO, CORY, HARGREAVES AND GAVITCH
400 WEST BROADWAY
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101

TELEPHONE
619-236-1414

Members:
WILLIAM A. PROCOPIO
ROBERT J. BUTTERFIELD, JR.
MICHAEL C. CORY
JAMES R. HARGREAVES
LEWIS J. GAVITCH
DENNIS A. [illegible]
JOHN O. VANDEN
FREDERICK M. KNAPP
MERLIN C. M. SHAE—JR.
DAVID B. SCOTT
ROBERT J. LOMBARDS
ANTHONY P. MARSIT
STEVEN R. LOURDES
JAMES S. VANDEN
MICHAEL T. MOLONDO
THOMAS J. LAUBE
DANIEL C. BLUMENTHAL
JAMES T. PAPPAS
STEVEN M. STAPLES
CRAIG R. BATIN

FAX 234-2502

H. BARLING TIC PALMER, JR.
ALAN H. PERRAS
FRANK WOOD
ROBERT J. PHILLIPS
JAMES B. PALMER
EDWARD FLEURIMAN
GERALD N. PINSON
MICHAEL J. POWELL
ROBERT SWINDELL
ANDREW M. DELROY
JACK DUMOUSKA
WILLIAM V. FOWLER
MATTHEW W. JAQUE
B. LLOYD S. MORTON
HOWARD J. PATTERSON
THOMAS J. BARRON
HO LEE L. OUREMAN

December 5, 1990

Roger Krauel
City Attorney of Encinitas
5090 Shoreham Place, Suite 101
San Diego, California 92122

Re: San Disguito Financial Centar
Case No. 88-367DR-EIA

Dear Roger:

At a Planning Commission meeting in July of 1990 when the
EIR on the above project was considered, and again on November
27, 1990 when the Planning Commission decided to hear an appeal
on the above project, Commissioner Dean indicated his desire to
consider a study prepared by a consultant for the City regarding
a realignment of Saxony Road.

In July the Planning Commission continued the matter for a
month as a courtesy to Commissioner Dean in order for him to meet
with the project applicant, staff and the consultant. Again,
last week the Commission gave another courtesy vote to
Commissioner Dean to hear the appeal brought by Mr. Pardee based
on that same issue, i.e. the potential realignment of Saxony
Road.

We have subsequently learned that Commissioner Dean has done
public relations work for the San Disguito National Bank within
the last twelve months. It is our understanding that he has been
paid $1,500 for this work. This realignment issue could have a
significant deleterious effect on the bank's leasehold interest.
If the realignment of Saxony Road were approved, the current
alignment of Saxony Road would end in a cul de sac before it
reaches Encinitas Boulevard. The bank would lose its frontage on
a through road. One potential new alignment of Saxony Road would
have it going through the current leasehold interest of the bank.
Under any circumstances, the new alignment would interfere with

LAW OFFICES OF

PROCOPIO, CORY, MARGREAVES AND SAVITCH

Roger Krauel
December 5, 1990
Page 2


the bank's drive-through operation and would reduce the ability
to "stack" cars in drive-through lines.

Under the Political Reform Act a public official cannot
participate in a decision if he has a "financial interest" in the
decision (Gov.C.§ 87100). A public official has a "financial
interest" if he is involved $250 or more from a source of income
in the last twelve months and the decision will have a material
financial effect on that source of income (Gov.C.§ 87103). A
decision will have a material financial effect on a source of
income who owns a leasehold interest if it is:

Reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
result in a change in the actual use of
property within 300 feet of the leased
property and the change will enhance or
significantly decrease the use or enjoyment
of the leased property. (Cal.Admin. C. §§
18702.6(b) and 18702.4(c).)

Mr. Paul Schedler, the President of San Dieguito National
Bank indicated to us that the proposed realignment would
significantly decrease the bank's use or enjoyment of the leased
property. Under these circumstances, we believe that
Commissioner Dean should not be participating in this decision.

I would appreciate it if you would discuss this matter with
Commissioner Dean. If Commissioner Dean confirms that the
information we have is correct, I would expect that he would
abstain from further participation.

Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation in this matter.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
THOMAS J. HARRON

TJH:dd

DEAN
& Associates

PROPOSAL TO DEVELOP
A COMMUNICATION PROGRAM FOR
SAN DIEGUITO NATIONAL BANK'S
STERLING ACCESS
HOME EQUITY LOANS

JUNE 21, 1990


OVERVIEW

The foundation of our agency and the most important service we
provide our clients is assisting them with the development of
communication plans for their products and services.

The focus of this proposal is to outline a program that will work
toward creating and implementing a communication plan. The
program consists of three steps:

Research

Plan Development

Plan Implementation




Advertising • Marketing • Public Relations

765 Normandy • Encinitas, CA 92024 • Tel 619/944-7489 Fax 619/944-7315

City of
Encinitas

December 3, 1990


Thomas J. Harron
Procopio, Cory, Hargreaves and Savitch
1900 Union Bank Building
530 B Street
San Diego, California 92101-4469

RE: Your November 28, 1990, correspondence
    San Dieguito Financial Center
    88-367 DR/EI

Dear Mr. Harron:

Please find attached a copy of Chapter 1.12 of the City of
Encinitas Municipal Code which establishes the standards for
APPEALS.

The material received by the Planning Commission for the November
27, 1990, meeting was available for public review commencing
November 21, 1990, and was available at the meeting of November 27,
November 21, 1990, and was available at the meeting of November 21,
1990. The Commission received their agenda packet on November 21,
1990, and based their determination to hear the PARDEE appeal on
the information contained in that agenda packet. Since the
Pardee appeal, staff
Planning Commission determined to hear the PARDEE appeal, staff
will prepare an additional staff report for the administrative
hearing which we will set for January 17, 1991. The Planning
hearing which we will set for January 17, 1991. The Planning
Commission cannot consider the appeal prior to that time due to the
regularly scheduled meetings and the fact that noticing regulations
require a ten day newspaper notice which cannot be accommodated
necessitate a ten day newspaper notice which cannot be accommodated
since the Coast Dispatch which publishes legal notices for the City
since the Coast Dispatch, which publishes legal notices for the City
publishes on November 29, 1990; and any legal notice would have to
have been to them by November 26, 1990, prior to the Commission's
determination to hear the Pardee appeal.


BW/03/CL25-7423WPF (12/03/90-1)

Thomas J. Barron
Page 2
December 3, 1990

The Planning Commission certified the Environmental Impact Report
for this project on August 28, 1990. The project has
been reviewed by the Commission. The Saxony Road alignment study
prepared by Dokken Engineering for the City of Encinitas has not
been considered by the Planning Commission staff has maintained
and will maintain a firm stance at hearings, that the Dokken study cannot
be on Mr. Young's property until an alignment for Saxony Road (if
realignment does occur) is adopted by the City Council.

Section 1.12.060(D) of the Municipal Code limits issues and
information presented during oral presentations to issues raised
in the appeal and supporting documents including the staff report
for the initial consideration; the staff report dated November 18,
1990, discussed the placement for reciprocal access between the
Young and Pardee properties. Therefore, you may raise that issue
in your oral presentation to the Planning Commission.

Please be advised that the Old Encinitas Community Advisory Board
approved the referenced project on October 25, 1990, and the
January 17, 1991, hearing of the Pardee appeal of last approval is
consistent with Chapter 1.12 of the Municipal Code and Section
65905 of the Government Code. It is the belief of staff that your
reference to Section 65905 is not applicable in this instance since
the project has been approved and codes do stipulate that any
approval may be appealed and the appeal may be heard.

Also, please be advised that the staff report for the January 17,
1991, administrative hearing will be available at City Hall on
January 11, 1991, should you or your client wish to obtain a copy
of the report. If you wish to supply additional written
information pertaining to the appeal and consistent with Chapter
1.12, please submit such information to Tom Curriden, Associate
Planner, prior to January 10, 1991, for inclusion in the agenda
packet.

If you have additional concerns, you may contact the undersigned
at (619) 944-5064.

Sincerely,

Bill Weedman
City Planner

cc: Warren Shafer, City Manager
    Roger Krauel, City Attorney
    Tom Curriden, Associate Planner

BW/03/CL25-7413wp5 (12/03/90-1)

embedding.qa_text: December 5, 1990 Roger Krauel City Attorney of Encinitas 5090 Shorsham Place, Suite 101 San Diego, California 92122 Re: San Dieguito Financial Center Case No. 88-367DR-EIA Dear Roger: At a Planning Commission meeting in July of 1990 when the EIR on the above project was considered, and again on November 27, 1990 when the Planning Commission decided to hear an appeal on the above project, Commissioner Dean indicated his desire to consider a study prepared by a consultant for the City regarding a realignment of Saxony Road. In July the Planning Commission continued the matter for a month as a courtesy to Commissioner Dean in order for him to meet with the project applicant, staff and the consultant. Again, last week the Commission gave another courtesy vote to Commissioner Dean to hear the appeal brought by Mr. Pardee based on that same issue, i.e. the potential realignment of Saxony Road. We have subsequently learned that Commissioner Dean has done public relations work for the San Dieguito National Bank within the last twelve months. It is our understanding that he has been paid $1,500 for this work. This realignment issue could have a significant deleterious effect on the bank's leasehold interest. If the realignment of Saxony Road were approved, the current alignment of Saxony Road would end in a cul de sac before it reaches Encinitas Boulevard. The bank would lose its frontage on a through road. One potential new alignment of Saxony Road would have it going through the current leasehold interest of the bank. Under any circumstances, the new alignment would interfere with the bank's drive-through operation and would reduce the ability to "stack" cars in drive-through lines. Under the Political Reform Act a public official cannot participate in a decision if he has a "financial interest" in the decision (Gov.C.§ 87100). A public official has a "financial interest" if he has received $250 or more from a source of income in the last twelve months and the decision will have a material financial effect on that source of income (Gov.C.§ 87103). A decision will have a material financial effect on a source of income who owns a leasehold interest if it is: Reasonably foreseeable that the decision will result in a change in the actual use of property within 300 feet of the leased property and the change will enhance or significantly decrease the use or enjoyment of the leased property. (Cal.Admin. C. §§ 18702.6(b) and 18702.4(c).) Mr. Paul Schedler, the President of San Diequito National Bank has indicated to us that the proposed realignment would significantly decrease the bank's use or enjoyment of the leased property. Under these circumstances it is our belief that Commissioner Dean should not be participating in this decision. I would appreciate it if you would discuss this matter with Commissioner Dean. If Commissioner Dean confirms that the information we have is correct, I would expect that he would abstain from further participation. Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation in this matter.
